mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity that boosts corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during months with rising PAYEMS over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy dominates discount rate effects and suppresses equity risk appetite, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction of accelerating consumer price inflation and decelerating industrial production generates stagflationary risk that reduces equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term between CPIAUCSL and INDPRO is below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions with unemployment below 4%, a subsequent rise in the unemployment rate signals the onset of labor market deterioration that precedes earnings downgrades, generating negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises > 0.2pp and baseline UNRATE < 4%, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales indicates weakening consumer demand that translates into lower corporate revenue expectations, producing negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are nonnegative in more than 50% of months with month-over-month declining RSAFS over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity utilization that supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during periods of rising INDPRO over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1,400 thousand units, acute contraction in residential investment signals broader demand deterioration that drags S&P 500 returns lower over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST < 1400 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a rising federal funds rate and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate confirms that monetary tightening is successfully slowing the labor market, increasing recession probability and producing negative 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the FEDFUNDS-UNRATE interaction term falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
